title: How PCR Finds Tiny DNA Traces
script_summary: Exploring how PCR amplifies tiny DNA traces to make them detectable.

segments.voiceover: Ever wonder how scientists find tiny DNA traces?
It's all thanks to PCR, or Polymerase Chain Reaction.
PCR exponentially copies DNA, making even the smallest samples detectable.
Imagine starting with just one tiny fragment; PCR can replicate it into millions!
This process helps identify genetic material in forensics, diseases, and ancestry.
Curious to learn more about molecular biology? Hit like and follow for more science insights!
